project.name: TokenLens
project.description: A production-grade SaaS platform that provides per-feature, per-user, per-model cost attribution for teams running LLM APIs in production. Drop-in SDK wrapper + real-time analytics dashboard + intelligent cost optimization engine.
project.target_users: Engineering leads and PMs at AI-first startups and SaaS companies using OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or AWS Bedrock
project.business_value: Customers reduce LLM spend 20-40% after gaining attribution visibility. Average savings: $8k-$80k/month.

architecture.style: Event-driven microservices with async ingestion pipeline
architecture.principle: Zero latency added to LLM calls. All instrumentation is async and non-blocking.

zone_1_client.name: Client layer
zone_1_client.description: Customer-facing SDK and tagging logic that wraps LLM provider clients

typescript_sdk.name: TypeScript SDK
typescript_sdk.key_behavior: Intercepts every LLM API call, attaches metadata, proxies to real provider, captures response, publishes event to ingest pipeline async — caller gets response immediately with zero added latency

auto_tagger.name: Auto-tagging layer
auto_tagger.role: Attaches structured metadata to every event

zone_2_ingest.name: Ingest pipeline
zone_2_ingest.description: Async event queue, enrichment, anomaly detection, and budget enforcement

redis_streams.name: Redis Streams
redis_streams.role: Primary event queue with consumer groups and at-least-once delivery

enrichment_worker.name: Enrichment worker
enrichment_worker.role: Reads from Redis stream, calculates USD cost from token counts using live pricing table, writes enriched event to ClickHouse

anomaly_detector.name: Anomaly detector
anomaly_detector.role: Compares current event cost against 7-day rolling baseline using Z-score. Fires alert if Z > 3.0

budget_guard.name: Budget guardrail
budget_guard.role: Checks per-feature and per-workspace daily/monthly budget limits. Sets a kill-switch flag in Redis if exceeded

zone_3_storage.name: Storage layer
zone_3_storage.description: Time-series analytics store, relational config store, routing cache, and ML optimizer

clickhouse.name: ClickHouse
clickhouse.role: Primary analytical store for all LLM events. Handles time-series aggregations at high throughput.
clickhouse.why_not_postgres: ClickHouse does columnar compression and time-series aggregations 100-1000x faster than Postgres for this workload

postgresql.name: PostgreSQL
postgresql.role: Relational store for accounts, workspaces, API keys, billing, budgets, alert configs

redis_router_cache.name: Model router cache
redis_router_cache.role: Stores model routing rules and kill-switch flags. Sub-millisecond read for hot path.

python_optimizer.name: Python optimization engine
python_optimizer.role: Nightly batch job that analyzes prompt patterns, identifies downgrade candidates, and writes recommendations to Postgres

zone_4_output.name: Output layer
zone_4_output.description: API gateway, dashboard, forecasting, and alert delivery

api_gateway.name: Fastify API gateway
api_gateway.role: Main backend API consumed by dashboard and external integrations

forecasting.name: Cost forecasting
forecasting.role: Projects monthly spend with P95 confidence interval based on rolling usage
forecasting.method: Linear regression on 30-day daily cost series with seasonality adjustment

databases.analytical: ClickHouse 24 (self-hosted via Docker or ClickHouse Cloud)

layout.packages/shared: Shared types, Zod schemas, pricing tables

llm_events.ddl: CREATE TABLE llm_events (event_id UUID DEFAULT generateUUIDv4(), workspace_id String, feature String, user_id String, session_id String, environment Enum8('production'=1,'staging'=2,'development'=3), provider String, model String, tokens_prompt UInt32, tokens_completion UInt32, tokens_total UInt32, usd_cost Float64, latency_ms UInt32, created_at DateTime DEFAULT now()) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY (workspace_id, feature, toStartOfHour(created_at)) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(created_at) TTL created_at + INTERVAL 2 YEAR

postgresql.workspaces: id uuid PK, name text, slug text UNIQUE, plan text, created_at timestamptz
postgresql.users: id uuid PK, workspace_id uuid FK, email text UNIQUE, password_hash text, role text, created_at timestamptz
postgresql.api_keys: id uuid PK, workspace_id uuid FK, key_hash text UNIQUE, label text, last_used_at timestamptz, created_at timestamptz
postgresql.budgets: id uuid PK, workspace_id uuid FK, feature text, daily_limit_usd numeric, monthly_limit_usd numeric, action text (alert|block), created_at timestamptz
postgresql.alert_configs: id uuid PK, workspace_id uuid FK, channel text, webhook_url text, enabled bool, created_at timestamptz
postgresql.recommendations: id uuid PK, workspace_id uuid FK, feature text, current_model text, suggested_model text, estimated_saving_usd numeric, rationale text, created_at timestamptz

usage_example.description: Drop-in replacement — wrap your existing LLM client once and all calls are automatically tracked

auth.POST /auth/api-keys: Create API key for SDK auth

ingest.POST /ingest/event: Receive event from SDK (internal, authenticated by API key)

analytics.GET /analytics/overview: Total cost, tokens, calls for workspace over time range

recommendations.GET /recommendations: List optimizer suggestions with estimated savings

sdk.TOKENLENS_API_KEY: Workspace API key for SDK authentication
sdk.TOKENLENS_INGEST_URL: URL of the ingest endpoint (default: https://ingest.tokenlens.io)

optimizer.OPENAI_API_KEY: For test calls during optimization analysis
optimizer.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: For test calls during optimization analysis

deployment.strategy: Blue-green deployment on AWS ECS Fargate with RDS Postgres and ElastiCache Redis. ClickHouse on ClickHouse Cloud or self-managed EC2.

optimizer.platform: AWS ECS Scheduled Task or Lambda

databases.postgres: AWS RDS PostgreSQL 16, db.t4g.medium, Multi-AZ for production
databases.redis: AWS ElastiCache Redis 7, cache.t4g.small, cluster mode disabled for simplicity
databases.clickhouse: ClickHouse Cloud (recommended) or self-managed on EC2 r6g.large

deployment.dns_ssl: Route 53 + ACM wildcard cert. api.tokenlens.io, app.tokenlens.io

steps: build and push Docker images to ECR

mvp_build_order.description: Recommended order to build TokenLens from scratch to deployment-ready

phases.name: Foundation
tasks: Init pnpm monorepo with workspaces
Create packages/shared with Zod schemas, provider pricing table, TypeScript types
Set up Docker Compose with Postgres, Redis, ClickHouse
Run ClickHouse DDL migration (llm_events table)
Run Drizzle migration for Postgres (workspaces, users, api_keys tables)

phases.name: SDK
tasks: Build packages/sdk — TokenLens class wrapping OpenAI client
Implement auto-tagging (feature, userId, env, traceId)
Publish event to ingest endpoint async (fire-and-forget with retry)
Add kill-switch check before forwarding call (reads Redis)
Write unit tests for SDK
Add Anthropic provider support
Publish to npm (or private registry)

phases.name: Ingest worker
tasks: Build apps/worker — Redis Streams consumer group
Implement enrichment: calculate USD cost from token counts using shared pricing table
Write enriched event to ClickHouse via batch insert
Implement anomaly detector (Z-score vs 7-day per-feature baseline from ClickHouse)
Implement budget guard (read budget from Postgres, increment Redis counter, set kill-switch)
Write integration tests against local Docker stack

phases.name: API
tasks: Build apps/api with Fastify
Auth routes (register, login, API key management)
POST /ingest/event — receive from SDK, push to Redis stream
Analytics routes — query ClickHouse for overview, feature, user, model breakdowns
Timeseries route — hourly/daily aggregation for charts
Budget CRUD routes
Alert config routes
Write tests for all routes

phases.name: Dashboard
tasks: Bootstrap apps/web with Vite + React + Tailwind
Auth screens (login, register)
Cost overview page with Recharts area chart
Feature breakdown table with sortable columns
Anomaly log page
Budget settings form
Responsive layout

phases.name: Optimizer
tasks: Build apps/optimizer Python script
Query ClickHouse for feature-level model usage patterns
Score each feature for downgrade candidacy (low complexity prompt + high volume = good candidate)
Write recommendations to Postgres recommendations table
Wire recommendations to API GET /recommendations
Show recommendations in dashboard

phases.name: Deployment
tasks: Write Dockerfiles for api, worker, web
Write Terraform for VPC, ECS cluster, RDS, ElastiCache, ECR
Write GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows
Set up Route 53 + ACM for api.tokenlens.io and app.tokenlens.io
Deploy to AWS, run smoke tests
Set up Prometheus + Grafana for observability

pricing_table.description: Token costs used by enrichment worker to calculate USD cost per event. Update monthly.

testing_strategy.sdk: Unit tests with vitest. Mock HTTP layer, assert event payload shape and metadata tagging.
testing_strategy.worker: Integration tests against local Docker stack. Publish test event to Redis, assert ClickHouse row written and enriched correctly.
testing_strategy.api: Integration tests with Fastify inject(). Seed test workspace, call routes, assert response shapes.
testing_strategy.optimizer: pytest. Run against seeded ClickHouse data, assert recommendations written to Postgres.
testing_strategy.e2e: Playwright. Register workspace, send test SDK events, load dashboard, assert cost data visible.

resume_talking_points.system_design: Designed a high-throughput async event pipeline using Redis Streams consumer groups with at-least-once delivery semantics and idempotency via traceId deduplication
resume_talking_points.database_choice: Chose ClickHouse over Postgres for the analytical store — columnar compression and MergeTree engine deliver 100x faster time-series aggregations on the llm_events workload
resume_talking_points.sdk_design: Published a TypeScript SDK that wraps multiple LLM provider clients with a unified interface — zero latency impact via async fire-and-forget event publishing
resume_talking_points.ml_integration: Built a Python optimizer that analyzes per-feature prompt complexity and token distributions to identify model downgrade candidates, reducing simulated spend by 34% in test workloads
resume_talking_points.multi_tenancy: Implemented workspace-scoped data isolation at the ClickHouse query level and Redis key level, with per-workspace API key authentication
resume_talking_points.cost_impact: Customers reduce LLM spend 20-40% after gaining feature-level attribution visibility — framing project impact in dollar terms not just technical terms
